comparison_with_prior_crest_runs.data_source: TKT-1024 / CHG-0963 metadata update (2026-07-21 ad-hoc comparison)

gemma4_31b_cloud_baseline.notes: High jitter (max-min 31.23s) likely from cloud-routing variance.

gemma4_26b_gguf_local.notes: Current committed default in scripts/crest-v1.3-judgment-benchmark.sh. ~5x faster than 31b-cloud, very low jitter.

gemma4_26b_mlx_local.per_call_mean_sec: not measured (script doesn't instrument per-call latency)
gemma4_26b_mlx_local.notes: TKT-1027 result. Same 20/20 score, but wall-clock ~2x slower than GGUF baseline. Accuracy is identical; wall-clock is the only meaningful difference.

comparison_with_prior_crest_runs.interpretation: All three model variants pass the CREST v1.3 judgment benchmark with identical 20/20 scores. The MLX variant matches the GGUF local baseline on accuracy but is ~2x slower on this workload. For CREST purposes (verifying whether an atom's evidence proves a post-condition), all three are equivalent in quality. The wall-clock gap is real but secondary — CREST runs are scheduled, not interactive. The committed default should remain gemma4:26b (GGUF) UNLESS Ken explicitly approves a swap; the MLX variant should be added to the model registry as a parallel option for workloads that benefit from its stronger small-prompt performance.

compliance_with_safety_rules.think_false_injection_required: yes (gemma4 family with Ollama 0.32.1 defaults to thinking=true; without think:false, num_predict budget gets consumed by chain-of-thought and response is empty with done_reason=length; see TKT-1017 / CHG-0956)

verdict: PASS — gemma4:26b-mlx scores 20/20 (1.00) on the CREST v1.3 judgment benchmark with think:false injection. Accuracy matches gemma4:26b (GGUF) and gemma4:31b-cloud. Wall-clock is ~2x slower than the GGUF local baseline (50s vs 24.64s) but the score parity means MLX is a viable production candidate for accuracy-bound workloads. Recommend: keep gemma4:26b (GGUF) as the committed CREST default; add gemma4:26b-mlx as a parallel option in the model registry; Ken to decide if MLX should replace GGUF for any specific workload based on the small/medium-prompt speed advantage (TKT-1027 capacity test shows +4-120% speed on those).
